body.title: [sprint] Design: produced_by backward edge + ONE escalation verb + dispatch-to-producer rule
body.description:
intention: I intend that every emission carries a backward edge so failure escalates to its producer and the graph corrects itself.

Every emission (ticket, decision, proof, note, config, skill output) must carry a backward edge to its producer, and 'couldn't solve' must escalate to the PRODUCER'S review (escalate-to-design), not the admin queue. This ticket is the design: (1) produced_by field semantics on every store envelope (additive, one field, no per-type schemes); (2) the single escalation verb shape ('artifact failed downstream: how'); (3) the dispatch rule — failure routes to the producing artifact's review, recursively; (4) the retired-producer case (escalate to producer's producer / owning decision); (5) integration with D-dev-pipeline-stations-2026-07-07 stations; (6) reconciliation with tier escalation (D-dicksimnel-escalation-chain-2026-06-10, T-inference-escalation-ladder-family-then-architect): tier = capability miss, produced_by = spec miss; (7) Granny-sole-writer constraint (D-granny-sole-ticket-writer-2026-07-07): any ticket-state mutation goes through her write-path.

**Affected files:** devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/feedback-edges.json (new; design artifact only — no code this ticket)

**Design rules:** one mechanism parameterized by addressee (homogeneity over special-case); canonical-store-only home

**Scope boundary:** design artifact ONLY; no code edits; implementation is T-produced-by-emission-sweep + T-escalation-dispatch-producer-review (gated on this). If design lands the escalation verb on the bus envelope (HIGH-inertia unseen_university/devices/bus/), STOP and get Akien pre-approval stamped into the impl ticket before any edit.

**Test plan:** no code tests — design ticket. Proof-on-close has no red form for design artifacts; close shipped-unproven naming the lever (a design-artifact completeness checker) unless a machine check exists by then.

**Completion criteria:** devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/feedback-edges.json exists and grep finds all 7 numbered sections above; decision D-feedback-edge-every-emission-2026-07-07 amended with a pointer to it.

**Validation:** grep -l feedback-edges.json + section-count check.

audit-emphasis: doc-only

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
